edge_hypothesis: All existing RSI pullback specs use RSI <= 40 or RSI <= 45 as the pullback threshold. This tests a fundamentally different entry philosophy: DEEP pullback entries at RSI <= 30 — the panic zone within a trend. The hypothesis: in crypto, RSI below 30 within an established uptrend (close > EMA_100) represents a PANIC SELL — institutions liquidating, retail panicking — that temporarily pushes price far below fair value. These are the highest-quality pullback entries because: (1) entry price is at maximum distance from trend EMA, maximizing the snap-back distance, (2) the panic creates a volatility spike (ATR expansion) that provides large absolute-dollar stop/TP distances, (3) at 10:1 R:R, only the explosive snap-back moves reach TP — these are the 'rubber band' trades where price has stretched too far from equilibrium and snaps back violently. The trade-off is DRASTICALLY fewer entries: RSI < 30 in an uptrend is rare (maybe 10-20 times per year on 1h). But each entry is at a point of maximum dislocation — the highest edge per trade. Combined with EMA_100 (17-day trend on 1h) instead of EMA_200 (33 days), we catch trends faster to get more of these rare setups. EMA_21 slope confirmation ensures the short-term trend was rising BEFORE the panic (not entering a genuine trend reversal). Macro alignment: in the current ranging/bear environment, panic dips within range-bound uptrends are exactly the setup that should produce sharp bounces — price dips to range support, RSI tanks, then bounces back to range midpoint/resistance. Invalidation: if trade count < 10 over the backtest period, the signal is too rare to be useful. If PF < 1.0, the RSI < 30 readings in crypto trends are NOT panic — they're genuine trend exhaustion, and the 'snap-back' thesis is wrong.

variants.name: rsi_pullback_1h_deep30_10to1
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/1h with RSI <= 30 (DEEP panic zone), EMA_100 trend, EMA_21 slope, 10:1 R:R. This is the most extreme pullback depth + R:R combination ever tested. RSI <= 30 in an uptrend is rare — maybe 15-25 entries over a full backtest period. But each entry catches a panic dip at maximum dislocation from trend. At 10:1 R:R (20x ATR TP), only violent snap-backs reach target. Stop at 2.0x ATR provides the STOP_FLOOR mandate. EMA_100 (17 days on 1h) is faster than default EMA_200, catching trends 16 days earlier — critical for getting enough setups at RSI <= 30. This is the frontier test: if PF > 1.5 at 10:1 with deep pullbacks, the system should explore RSI <= 25 and 15:1 R:R. If PF < 1.0, deep pullbacks don't snap back hard enough on 1h — the dislocation is too brief at this timeframe.
entry_long: Close > EMA_100 (price above 100-period EMA — in an established uptrend, fast enough to catch crypto trends).
EMA_21 slope is rising (EMA_21 now > EMA_21 previous bar — short-term trend was bullish BEFORE the panic dip).
RSI_14 <= 30 (DEEP oversold — panic pullback within uptrend, maximum dislocation from equilibrium).
entry_short: Close < EMA_100 (price below 100-period EMA — in an established downtrend).
EMA_21 slope is falling (EMA_21 now < EMA_21 previous bar — short-term trend was bearish).
RSI_14 >= 70 (DEEP overbought — panic rally within downtrend, symmetric deep entry).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (20.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if trade count < 10, signal is too rare — widen to RSI <= 35. If PF < 1.0, deep pullbacks don't snap back at 1h — test 4h variant.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 20.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_100 fast trend filter + EMA_21 slope — catches crypto trends 16 days earlier than EMA_200
RSI_14 <= 30 — DEEP panic pullback, maximum dislocation from trend equilibrium
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 20.0x TP — 10:1 R:R extreme tail harvesting on panic entries

variants.name: rsi_pullback_4h_deep30_8to1
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/4h with RSI <= 30, EMA_100 trend, EMA_21 slope, 8:1 R:R. The 4h timeframe variant. On 4h, RSI <= 30 within uptrend is even rarer — maybe 5-12 entries over backtest period. But each 4h RSI < 30 reading represents a multi-day panic: 14 bars of RSI at 4h = 56 hours of data producing an oversold reading. These are MAJOR dislocations — flash crashes, cascading liquidations, black swan dips within secular trends. At 8:1 R:R (16x ATR), the snap-back from these events needs to cover substantial ground, which major dislocations typically do. Lower R:R than 1h variant (8:1 vs 10:1) because 4h moves are larger in absolute terms but slower to develop. EMA_100 on 4h = 400 hours = 17 days — same real-time period as EMA_100 on 1h but with 4x the information density per bar.
entry_long: Close > EMA_100 (price above 4h 100-period EMA — major uptrend, 17 days of data).
EMA_21 slope is rising (EMA_21 now > EMA_21 previous bar — medium-term bullish momentum confirmed).
RSI_14 <= 30 (DEEP oversold on 4h — major multi-day panic within uptrend).
entry_short: Close < EMA_100 (price below 4h 100-period EMA — major downtrend).
EMA_21 slope is falling (EMA_21 now < EMA_21 previous bar — bearish momentum).
RSI_14 >= 70 (DEEP overbought on 4h — major panic rally in downtrend).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (16.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if trade count < 5, signal too rare on 4h for statistical validity — rely on 1h variant results instead
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 16.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_100 trend filter + EMA_21 slope — 17-day trend on 4h for macro alignment
RSI_14 <= 30 — DEEP panic on 4h, multi-day dislocation event
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 16.0x TP — 8:1 R:R on major panic entries

variants.name: rsi_pullback_1h_moderate35_7to1_control
variants.description: Control variant: RSI <= 35 on ETH/1h with EMA_100 trend, EMA_21 slope, 7:1 R:R. This is the middle ground between existing specs (RSI <= 40-45) and the deep panic variant (RSI <= 30). By testing RSI <= 35 at 7:1 R:R (same R:R as MACD champion), we get a clean comparison: (1) vs RSI <= 30 at 10:1 — does deeper entry quality compensate for fewer trades? (2) vs existing rp4f2w8n at RSI <= 45, 7:1 R:R with EMA_100 — does the tighter RSI filter from 45 to 35 improve signal quality enough to increase PF? This triangulation reveals the optimal RSI depth for pullback entries at the 7:1 R:R level.
entry_long: Close > EMA_100 (price above 100-period EMA — fast trend filter).
EMA_21 slope is rising (EMA_21 now > EMA_21 previous bar — short-term momentum confirmed).
RSI_14 <= 35 (moderate-deep pullback — tighter than default 40, wider than panic 30).
entry_short: EMA_21 slope is falling (EMA_21 now < EMA_21 previous bar — bearish momentum).
RSI_14 >= 65 (moderate-deep overbought — symmetric to long entry).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (14.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF between RSI<=30 variant and this — reveals optimal pullback depth
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 14.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_100 fast trend + EMA_21 slope — same setup as deep variant for controlled comparison
RSI_14 <= 35 — moderate-deep pullback, middle ground between default 40 and panic 30
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 14.0x TP — 7:1 R:R, same as MACD champion for cross-template comparison
